description: User needs step-by-step appeal guidance after first denial

conversation.content: My son's insurance denied his residential treatment. The letter says I can appeal within 180 days. I've never done this before. Can you walk me through what to do?

must_explain: get letter of medical necessity from treating provider

description: Internal appeal already failed, user needs to know about external review

conversation.content: We already did the internal appeal and they denied it again. Same reason — 'not medically necessary.' His therapist wrote a letter and everything. Is there anything else we can do or is this it?

must_explain: external review / independent review is available
external reviewer is independent from the insurance company
the insurance company's decision is not final
state insurance commissioner complaint is another option
must_not_say: there's nothing more you can do

description: Appeal deadline is tomorrow — extreme time pressure

conversation.content: I just realized the appeal deadline is TOMORROW. I completely missed it because I've been dealing with getting my daughter into a new facility. The denial letter is from 6 months ago. Can I still do anything?

must_explain: check exact deadline language in the letter
some plans allow late appeals with good cause
external review may have a separate deadline
state insurance commissioner complaint has its own timeline
call the insurance company to request an extension
must_not_say: you missed it, it's too late
